meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop building in the dark. Learn how to implement advanced analytics to escape tutorial hell and validate your SaaS metrics using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start measuring. By integrating event-driven analytics into your MVP, you replace guesswork with user behavior data. Use Antigravity to scaffold your telemetry layer and Claude Code to automate the instrumentation, ensuring every user action translates into actionable business intelligence for your SaaS.
content.intro:
You’re trapped in the infinite loop of 'build-deploy-repeat,' yet your dashboard remains a graveyard of zero-session metrics. You’ve mastered the framework, but you’re failing the market. The difference between a hobbyist and a founder isn't better code; it’s the ability to quantify user intent through rigorous analytics.

This isn't another generic walkthrough. We are stripping away the fluff and building a production-grade analytics pipeline that tracks conversion, churn, and feature adoption. By bridging Antigravity’s structural efficiency with Claude Code’s iterative power, you will transform your codebase from a collection of tutorials into a high-converting SaaS engine.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the three critical events that signal user success. This prevents 'data noise' and keeps your analytics focused on revenue-generating behaviors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current SaaS codebase and identify the core user journey. Generate a schema for event tracking (trackEvent function) that captures user signup, core feature usage, and subscription conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /review src/analytics/schema.ts --context 'Focus on event-driven architecture for SaaS'.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Telemetry Layer
steps.description: Implement a centralized analytics wrapper to decouple your business logic from specific providers like Mixpanel or PostHog.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a type-safe analytics provider wrapper in TypeScript that handles asynchronous event logging and includes a silent fallback for local development environments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Create a singleton AnalyticsService class that handles event buffering and batching'.

steps.title: Instrument High-Intent Features
steps.description: Inject tracking hooks into your core MVP features to measure friction points where users drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my existing feature components to inject the analytics service hook. Ensure every button interaction is tracked with user-specific metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit src/components/ --apply 'Inject analytics tracking on all CTA clicks'.

steps.title: Automate User Context Enrichment
steps.description: Ensure every event carries the necessary identity context (user ID, plan type, cohort) without manual code duplication.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a middleware for my API routes that automatically attaches user session metadata to every outgoing analytics event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate an Express middleware to extract JWT claims and inject them into the request analytics context'.

steps.title: Validate Data Integrity
steps.description: Establish a testing protocol to ensure your analytics events fire correctly before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests that verify event payloads match the schema defined in Step 1.

pro_tips: Never track everything. If you can't build a business decision based on a data point, don't collect it.
Use a 'Data Contract' file to define events; if the code doesn't match the contract, the build should fail.
Implement server-side tracking for core conversion events to bypass ad-blockers and browser privacy restrictions.
Always include a 'debug' mode flag that logs analytics payloads directly to the browser console for instant validation.
Store user cohorts in local storage to prevent redundant API calls for user properties on every page load.
content.next_steps: Deploy your telemetry layer to staging, trigger a series of test events, and verify the data flow in your analytics dashboard. Once confirmed, optimize your UI based on the drop-off points identified.
content.conversion_block: Stop building features nobody uses and start building a product that scales. By mastering analytics, you move from guessing to growing. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
